action: Navigate Discover, Portfolio, Create, pool actions, and legacy detached action routes.

expectedAssertions.statement: Primary navigation exposes Discover, Portfolio, and Create.
Swap, Create Position, and Withdraw operate inside an exact pool workspace.
Legacy routes resolve only into a safe exact-pool context and never choose an ambiguous pool.

negativePaths.trigger: A detached action route lacks exact pool identity or matches multiple pools.

prerequisites: Multiple pools exist for one token pair.
recovery: Return to Discover and select the exact pool.
Keep invalid routes read-only until selection.
title: Navigation keeps actions pool-scoped

action: Use Back, Forward, deep links, and reload before submission, during pending, after replacement, and after canonical completion.

expectedAssertions.statement: Navigation preserves safe pool, action, view, filter, and page context.
Unsubmitted quotes and drafts cannot execute after reload without fresh preflight.
Pending and replacement hashes plus canonical History persist without replaying a wallet request.
Terminal banners do not leak into unrelated routes or new drafts.

negativePaths.trigger: Reload occurs with an unsubmitted reviewed quote.
Reload occurs while pending or after replacement.

prerequisites: Browser history contains multiple action states.
recovery: Resume the submitted intent without another prompt.
title: Browser history preserves safe state without replay

action: Use only keyboard input through navigation, financial forms, menus, sheets, dialogs, and transaction controls; close overlays with Escape.

expectedAssertions.statement: Every interactive control is reachable and operable in logical order with visible focus.
Overlays receive focus, contain it appropriately, close with Escape, and return focus to their invoker.
No loading, validation, wallet, or transaction state traps focus or sends it to hidden content.

negativePaths.trigger: An overlay closes after error, wallet rejection, route change, or Escape.

prerequisites: Desktop and mobile overlays are renderable.
recovery: Continue traversal from the restored position.
title: Keyboard interaction has visible focus and no traps

action: Inspect accessible names, descriptions, roles, live announcements, chart tables, and bin-state cues across validation and transaction states.

expectedAssertions.statement: Controls, instructions, errors, and statuses have programmatic names and relationships.
Pending, success, and reverted changes are announced without duplicate announcements.
Charts expose equivalent tables and bin states use text or shape in addition to color.

negativePaths.trigger: Color perception or visual chart interpretation is unavailable.

recovery: Expose state through associated text and live regions.
title: Financial state has programmatic equivalents

action: Render critical routes at 320 CSS pixels and 200% zoom with long addresses, balances, minima, hashes, and errors.

expectedAssertions.statement: Identity, review fields, and submit or recovery controls remain operable without two-dimensional page scrolling.
Long values wrap or truncate without overlap, clipping, or horizontal page overflow.
Sticky and overlay controls do not cover required content.

negativePaths.trigger: Maximum-length values render at the narrowest width and 200% zoom.

prerequisites: Core route and error fixtures exist.
recovery: Keep primary action reachable through one-dimensional scrolling.
title: Critical controls survive narrow width and zoom

action: Operate mobile navigation, action rail, sheets, review, and wallet handoff using touch; return from wallet accept and reject outcomes.

expectedAssertions.statement: Touch targets satisfy supported target-size or spacing rules without overlapping activation.
The action rail works as an accessible mobile surface while retaining pool and draft context.
After wallet acceptance or rejection, focus returns to the actionable pending, retry, or review control.

negativePaths.trigger: Dense mobile actions render near each other.

prerequisites: Mobile viewport and touch measurement are available.
recovery: Return focus to Retry or fresh review.
Reopen the action surface without losing the pool.
title: Mobile actions and wallet focus remain usable

action: Render loading, empty, error, partial, pending, success, and reverted states for core routes on desktop and mobile.

expectedAssertions.statement: Each state uses one restrained shared presentation with specific status and next action.
Mutually exclusive terminal panels never display simultaneously and placeholder copy is absent.
Content and controls are not clipped or hidden.
Retry cannot let a stale asynchronous completion overwrite the newer generation.

negativePaths.trigger: A transaction reaches a terminal state while another source remains partial.
A slow request completes after a newer retry.

title: Shared states are restrained and unambiguous

action: Inspect core routes with reduced motion, contrast checks, dense values, missing token icons, and responsive chart themes.

expectedAssertions.statement: Reduced motion suppresses nonessential animation without hiding state or focus.
Text, controls, focus, charts, and states retain readable contrast.
Dense-number formatting preserves precision, sign, units, and token identity.
Missing icons use a readable non-spoofing fallback and chart theming remains legible.

negativePaths.trigger: An icon fails amid extreme and unavailable numeric values.

prerequisites: Preference and contrast inspection are available.
Fixtures include dense values and missing assets.
recovery: Use static state changes and persistent focus.
Render identity fallback and exact value text.
title: Quiet Carbon remains readable under visual preferences
